notes: Helen Stoner arrives at Baker Street at dawn in a state of visible fear, and Holmes takes the case immediately — one of the few instances in the canon where he does not question the client's credibility before acting. He travels to Stoke Moran to investigate, spends a night in Julia's old room, and confronts the danger directly. Helen's courage in coming to Baker Street at all — given Roylott's violence — impresses Holmes. The case is one of the clearest examples in the canon of Holmes using his abilities explicitly to protect a client rather than simply to solve a puzzle.
